titlestmt.titleproper: Bronfenbrenner Center for Translational Research records23-27-4418

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-10-30 18:17:13 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Bronfenbrenner Center for Translational Research records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Bronfenbrenner Center for Translational Research. New York State College of Human Ecology

did.abstract: Translational research podcasts by various Cornell faculty.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Bronfenbrenner Center for Translational Research (BCTR) was created in 2011 and it consolidated and expanded programs and resources previously housed in the Family Life Development Center (FLDC) and the Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center (BLCC). The BCTR fosters research to understand and discover solutions to human problems across multiple levels of analysis, as well as to identify, evaluate and disseminate evidence-based and cost-efficient practices and programs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Bronfenbrenner Center for Translational Research records collection contains brochures and flyers of various talks and events. Also included are translational research podcasts by various Cornell faculty.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Bronfenbrenner Center for Translational Research records, #23-27-4418. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/11731

did.unittitle: BCTR podcasts

Ep. 2 - Charles Izzo
Ep. 3 - Andrew Turner, NY State 4-H Leader

Ep. 5 - Youth and Purpose with Anthony Burrow, Human Development, Cornell
Ep. 6 - Mass Incarceration and Inequality with Christopher Wildeman, Policy Analysis & Management, Cornell
Ep. 7 - Talk to Your Child with Marianella Casasola
Ep. 8 - Evaluating Military Family Programs with Brian Leidy
Ep. 9 - Research Community Partnerships with Jennifer Agans, PRYDE, Cornell
Ep. 10 - Architecture is a Social Art with Mardelle Shepley, Design & Environmental Analysis, Cornell
Ep. 11 - Incarceration is a Family Issue with Megan Comfort, Research Triangle Institute

Ep. 13 - Using Data to Help Children with Dana Weiner, Chapin Hall, University of Chicago
Ep. 14 - Mental Health Support in Palliative Care with Elissa Kozlov, Center for End-of-Life Research, Weill Cornell Medical College
Ep. 15 - Aging and Insecurity with Peter Lloyd-Sherlock, University of East Anglia
Ep. 16 - There is No Health System with Monika Safford, Weill Cornell Medical College
Ep. 17 - Cultivating Broader, Better Human Beings with Janis Whitlock, Cornell University
Ep. 18 - The Well Being of Children and Older Adults with Maria Fitzpatrick, Cornell University
Ep. 19 - Redeeming Teenage Killers with James Garbarino, Loyola University Chicago
Ep. 20 - What is Translational Research with John Eckenrode, Cornell University
Ep. 21 - Identity, Disparity, and Reaching Goals with Neil. A Lewis, Cornell University
Ep. 22 - Life in the Year After Prison with Bruce Western, Columbia University
Ep. 23 - Give All Children a Chance with Kimberly Kopko, Cornell University
Ep. 24 - Transforming Our Interactions wtih Nature with bethany ojalehto, Human Development, Cornell University
Ep. 25 - Social Media TestDrive for Youth with Amanda Purington, Cornell University
Ep. 26 - Changing How We Work with Phyllis Moen, University of Minnesota
Ep. 27 - Studying Implicit Bias with Melissa Ferguson, Cornell University
Ep. 28 - Immigrant Workers' Rights with Shannon Gleeson, Cornell University
Ep. 29 - Public Opinion Pushing Criminal Justice Reform with Peter Enns, Cornell University
Ep. 30 - Inequality, Public Policy and Democracy with Jamila Michener, Cornell University
Ep. 31 - Working with State Agencies to Improve Child Wellbeing with Lonnie Berger, University of Wisconsin - Madison
Ep. 32 - Adapting to Sudden Change with William Hobbs, Cornell University
Ep. 33 - Using Data to Improve the Criminal Justice System with Lars Andersen, Rockwool Foundation
Ep. 34 - Communities Have Knowledge with Bruce Lewenstein, Cornell University
Ep. 35 - When Does Surveillance Cross a Line with Karen Levy, Cornell University
Ep. 36 - The Issues of Rural America with Dan Lichter, Policy Analysis & Management, Cornell University
Ep. 37 - The Use and Impact of Digital Learning with Ren√© Kizilcec, Cornell University
Ep. 38 - How We Misunderstand Influence and Consent with Vanessa Bohns, Cornell University
Ep. 39 - Protecting Children in Care with Martha Holden, BCTR, Cornell University
Ep. 40 - Transforming the Health of (Post-)Incarcerated Patients with Emily Wang, Yale University

Ep. 42 - Youth are Assets, not Problems with Jane Powers, Cornell University
